settings.title: Spring Back to the Basics Challenge
settings.excerpt: A four week reset focused on technique, sleep, and walking, with no crash diets in sight.
settings.body:
Spring tends to bring a wave of guilt about winter habits, and with it a lot of extreme plans that fall apart by week two. We are offering something calmer.

The Ironform Back to Basics challenge runs through May. It is built on three boring, effective things: lift twice a week with good form, walk most days, and protect your sleep.

Why boring works

Extreme plans fail because they ask for everything at once. The body and the calendar both push back. A few small habits, held steadily for a month, change far more than a frantic fortnight ever does. The aim here is to finish May still doing the things you started.

We will check in each week, track a couple of simple numbers, and adjust as you go. No shakes to buy, no foods banned.

The three things we ask

The whole challenge fits on a postcard, which is rather the idea:

  • Two short strength sessions a week, with form before weight.
  • A walk on most days, even a short one after dinner.
  • A regular sleep window you actually defend.

A small tip from past rounds: pick the same two days each week for your sessions and treat them like any other appointment. People who decide in advance show up far more often than people who wait to feel motivated.

It is free to current members and open to a handful of newcomers. Drop in and ask if you would like one of the outside spots.
